mechanism_of_action: Inhibits cyclooxygenase (COX) enzymes, primarily COX-2, in the central nervous system, reducing prostaglandin synthesis from arachidonic acid to mediate analgesic and antipyretic effects with minimal peripheral anti-inflammatory activity.

pharmacokinetics.absorption: Rapidly absorbed from the gastrointestinal tract with bioavailability of 70-90%. Onset of action within 30-60 minutes and peak plasma concentrations at 1-2 hours.
pharmacokinetics.metabolism: Primarily hepatic via glucuronidation (45-55%) and sulfation (25-35%); minor CYP2E1 pathway produces NAPQI metabolite.
pharmacokinetics.elimination: Excreted primarily in urine as inactive conjugates; renal route predominates.

approved_indications: Relief of mild to moderate pain
clinical_use.standard_dosing: Adults: 325-650 mg orally every 4-6 hours as needed, maximum 4 g daily.

dose_adjustments.hepatic_impairment: Reduce dose or avoid in severe hepatic impairment
dose_adjustments.renal_impairment: Extend dosing interval in severe renal impairment

drug_interactions.mechanism: Pharmacodynamic: possible additive effects on coagulation or inhibition of warfarin metabolism
drug_interactions.clinical_effect: Increased INR and risk of bleeding
drug_interactions.management: Monitor INR frequently and adjust warfarin dose as necessary

drug_interactions.mechanism: Pharmacokinetic: CYP enzyme induction increasing formation of toxic NAPQI metabolite
drug_interactions.management: Monitor liver function tests and consider alternative analgesics

drug_interactions.mechanism: Pharmacokinetic: CYP enzyme induction may reduce paracetamol efficacy
drug_interactions.management: Monitor pain control and adjust paracetamol dose if needed
